concept: Energy / Active Force / Power to Act
description: The capacity for action and work; the force that enables movement and change. 'Kenua' names what powers activity—the resource that transforms intention into action.
sound_symbolism: The word kindles with force. 'Ke' activates with kindling spark, energy gathering. 'Nu' nurtures with sustained momentum, power building. Then 'a' opens to available flow.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo kenua' (the energy). What makes 'lori' (vital) possible. Powers 'teru' (process) and all action.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Renewable energy—sustainable power from sun, wind, and living systems.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Energy as chi—the life force that flows through all beings and actions.
